name: Endpoint Validity appraisal for Tad Pde5 Class Transfer Agent Directness bundle
summary: Accepted. The endpoints are valid for an evidence-directness framework: mechanism, pharmacokinetics, PDE selectivity, label safety boundaries, and agent classification. The claim does not convert those framework endpoints into clinical outcome claims.

findings.claim_or_issue: Mechanism, half-life, nitrate timing, PDE selectivity, and label warning endpoints are appropriate for class-transfer rules.
findings.why_it_matters: The bundle is not trying to answer whether tadalafil improves any off-label outcome.
findings.recommended_action: No correction required; keep outcome-specific endpoints in separate review-question baselines.

findings.claim_or_issue: The framework claim avoids using pharmacology endpoints as proof of cognition, mood, cardiovascular, exercise, MCI, or longevity benefit.
findings.why_it_matters: Mechanistic endpoints are a common source of overreach in off-label synthesis.
findings.recommended_action: No correction required; preserve this boundary in public copy.
